name: Alliance
description_short: Alliance Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution offering auto loans, mortgages, checking/savings accounts, business solutions, and personal loans with competitive rates.
description_long:
Alliance Credit Union is a federally-insured credit union that serves individuals and businesses as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution. The organization emphasizes accessibility, allowing membership with a minimum $5 savings account deposit and no membership fees. According to their website, they recently completed a merger with P&G St. Louis Employees Credit Union as of October 2024, expanding their membership base and service offerings.

Alliance offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (up to 84 months), mortgages (fixed, adjustable, and first-time homebuyer programs), personal loans (up to $20,000), business checking and loans, credit cards, and deposit accounts. They provide both consumer and business banking solutions, including a Greenlight debit card product for teens and children with parental controls. The credit union operates physical branches and ATMs alongside digital banking through their online platform and "Virtual Branch" access.

The organization distinguishes itself through emphasis on local decision-making, competitive rates, and transparency (advertising "no hidden fees"). They highlight flexible loan terms, fast approval processes, and comprehensive member support. Their messaging focuses on simplicity and member financial empowerment, offering financial literacy resources and security awareness content on their website.

As a credit union, Alliance operates under NCUA insurance rather than FDIC, serving member-owners rather than external shareholders. Their service quality and rates are typical for mid-sized credit unions. The website provides limited specific rate information, requiring members to check current rates directly. The organization's recent merger suggests ongoing growth and evolution of their service model.
services: Auto loans with terms up to 84 months
Greenlight debit cards for teens with parental controls
Online banking and Virtual Branch access
pros: Low minimum deposit requirement ($5) with no membership fees makes joining accessible
Auto loans with flexible terms up to 84 months and advertised competitive rates
Local, fast decision-making on loan applications rather than centralized processing
No hidden fees policy explicitly stated across multiple loan products
Comprehensive product range including auto, mortgage, personal loans, business accounts, and credit cards
Physical branch network plus digital banking and Shared Branch access for convenience
Greenlight debit card for teens with parental money management tools
cons: Website provides minimal specific rate information; customers must contact branch or login to see actual rates
Limited details on business loan products, terms, and eligibility requirements
Recent merger (October 2024) may create service integration or transition issues for new members
best_for: Auto loan borrowers seeking flexible terms and local decision-making
First-time homebuyers exploring mortgage options with personalized guidance
Small business owners needing checking, loans, and credit card solutions from a member-focused institution
Families wanting parental control tools for teaching teens financial management
diagnosis: Alliance Credit Union is best for individuals and business owners seeking member-owned financial services with flexible lending terms, local decision-making, and no hidden fees. The main caveat is that specific rates and terms are not published on their website, requiring direct contact with a branch or online login to compare products against competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Alliance — Credit Union in Fenton, MO | CreditDoc
meta_description: Alliance — NCUA-insured credit union in Fenton, MO. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
